title: DDPM Reverse Diffusion
learning_objective: Explain how DDPMs define a forward noising process and learn a reverse process that gradually denoises samples.
source_excerpt: Diffusion probabilistic models use a latent variable chain inspired by nonequilibrium thermodynamics. The forward process adds Gaussian noise over timesteps, while the learned reverse process denoises step by step.

symbol_ledger.visual_role: Structured point or image tile on the data manifold.
Particle or image tile increasingly covered by noise.

symbol_ledger.meaning: Noise variance schedule for the forward step.

causal_steps.plain_language: Start from a clean sample on the data manifold.
causal_steps.visual_metaphor: A green structured sample sits on a clean manifold ribbon.

causal_steps.plain_language: The forward process repeatedly adds Gaussian noise according to beta_t.
causal_steps.visual_metaphor: The sample diffuses into a purple noise cloud as time increases.

causal_steps.plain_language: At any timestep t, x_t can be sampled directly from x_0 using alpha_bar_t.
causal_steps.visual_metaphor: A time slider maps clean signal strength to noise variance.

causal_steps.plain_language: The learned reverse process removes predicted noise step by step.
causal_steps.visual_metaphor: Particles converge from noise cloud back toward a structured sample.

misconceptions.risk: Viewers may think the model jumps from noise to image in one step.
misconceptions.animation_guardrail: Show multiple reverse timesteps with gradual convergence.

misconceptions.risk: Viewers may think forward noising is learned.
misconceptions.animation_guardrail: Show beta_t as a fixed schedule and the reverse denoiser as learned.

misconceptions.risk: Viewers may interpret particle clouds as exact image pixels.
misconceptions.animation_guardrail: Mark them as low-dimensional teaching abstractions.
